chain.thought: Explanation: Team A must have riders who both outscored and were outscored by Team B’s rider. Euskaltel-Euskadi (Team A) has two riders with 7 and 3 points, while AG2R-La Mondiale (Team B) has one rider with 5 points. The 7-point rider outscored 5, and the 3-point rider was outscored by 5. Team A’s total is 10 (7+3), Team B’s total is 5, so Team A scored 5 more points than Team B.

judge_thought:
Explanation:
- The only team with two riders in the list is Euskaltel–Euskadi (Team A) at ranks 7 and 9, earning 7 and 3 points respectively (total 10).
- AG2R–La Mondiale (Team B) has a rider at rank 8 with 5 points, positioned between Euskaltel’s two riders.
- Thus, Team A both outscored (7 > 5) and was outscored by (3 < 5) Team B.
- Team A’s total (10) minus Team B’s total (5) equals 5.
